records.ety: [F. compliment. It complimento, fr. comlire to compliment, finish, suit, fr. L. complere to fill up. See Complete, and cf. Complement.]

defs: An expression, by word or act, of approbation, regard, confidence, civility, or admiration; a flattering speech or attention; a ceremonious greeting; as, to send one's compliments to a friend.
quotes: Tedious waste of time, to sit and hear So many hollow compliments and lies.

defs: To praise, flatter, or gratify, by expressions of approbation, respect, or congratulation; to make or pay a compliment to.
quotes: Monarchs should their inward soul disguise; . . . Should compliment their foes and shun their friends.

defs: To pass compliments; to use conventional expressions of respect.
quotes: I make the interlocutors, upon occasion, compliment with one another.
